g.text: * Two main LOGICAL buses are routed throughout the board:

g.desc: * Two main LOGICAL buses are routed throughout the board:

g.text: - USER: The USER bus is focused on controlling the board itself (SoC/MCU), e.g. setting up

g.desc: - USER: The USER bus is focused on controlling the board itself (SoC/MCU), e.g. setting up

g.text: configuration, issuing commands, retrieving collected data, etc. It has a single physical

g.desc: configuration, issuing commands, retrieving collected data, etc. It has a single physical

g.text: bus with at most two wires, and can be multi-protocol (one chosen at a time). It can be a

g.desc: bus with at most two wires, and can be multi-protocol (one chosen at a time). It can be a

g.text: multi-master bus, when configured as I2C.

g.desc: multi-master bus, when configured as I2C.

g.text: As a rule, in this bus, the board (SoC/MCU) acts as a SLAVE, but depending on the mode of

g.desc: As a rule, in this bus, the board (SoC/MCU) acts as a SLAVE, but depending on the mode of

g.text: operation, the bus can be used as a secondary PHER bus, or it can be just converted to

g.desc: operation, the bus can be used as a secondary PHER bus, or it can be just converted to

g.text: - PHER: The Pheripheral bus is where the devices the board controls directly are located;

g.desc: - PHER: The Pheripheral bus is where the devices the board controls directly are located;

g.text: e.g. the fuel gauge, battery charger, miscellaneous devices like screens or EEPROMs, etc.

g.desc: e.g. the fuel gauge, battery charger, miscellaneous devices like screens or EEPROMs, etc.

g.text: For this bus, the board (SoC/MCU) acts strictly as a MASTER, but it can be configured

g.desc: For this bus, the board (SoC/MCU) acts strictly as a MASTER, but it can be configured

g.text: to be multi-master. It is I2C-only.

g.desc: to be multi-master. It is I2C-only.

g.text: * The board operates in two distinct and fixed modes, programmable at the firmware level:

g.desc: * The board operates in two distinct and fixed modes, programmable at the firmware level:

g.text: - STANDALONE: The board is meant to run without supervision. It will be configured by default

g.desc: - STANDALONE: The board is meant to run without supervision. It will be configured by default

g.text: in SoC/MCU firmware, or if enabled, interactively via a pre-specified "HAT" (if supported;

g.desc: in SoC/MCU firmware, or if enabled, interactively via a pre-specified "HAT" (if supported;

g.text: e.g. additional EEPROM storage, screen with touch or buttons, etc.). In this mode, the board

g.desc: e.g. additional EEPROM storage, screen with touch or buttons, etc.). In this mode, the board

g.text: acts as MASTER in both USER and PHER buses.

g.desc: acts as MASTER in both USER and PHER buses.

g.text: - MANAGED: The board is meant to be controlled by a host system. The board acts as a MASTER

g.desc: - MANAGED: The board is meant to be controlled by a host system. The board acts as a MASTER

g.text: in the PHER bus, and as a SLAVE in the USER bus.

g.desc: in the PHER bus, and as a SLAVE in the USER bus.

g.text: * The physical buses for PHER/USER support specific bus types, selectable at firmware level:

g.desc: * The physical buses for PHER/USER support specific bus types, selectable at firmware level:

g.text: External strong pull-ups are always present, and pull to 3.3V.

g.desc: External strong pull-ups are always present, and pull to 3.3V.

g.text: See Pinouts on the table below.

g.desc: See Pinouts on the table below.

g.text: * To the MCU all lines are 5V-tolerant except for pins PA4 and PA5. However, try to avoid

g.desc: * To the MCU all lines are 5V-tolerant except for pins PA4 and PA5. However, try to avoid

g.text: * If the MCU is configured to sleep at Standby level, it will only wake up on ~{PHER1_IRQ1}

g.desc: * If the MCU is configured to sleep at Standby level, it will only wake up on ~{PHER1_IRQ1}

g.text: or ~{USER_IRQB} assertion (low-level, on falling edge). The pins PA0 and PA2 are configured

g.desc: or ~{USER_IRQB} assertion (low-level, on falling edge). The pins PA0 and PA2 are configured

g.text: as system wake-up pins at firmware level.

g.desc: as system wake-up pins at firmware level.

g.text: * The ~{BTN} is the general purpose User Button. It can be used by the host for any purpose.

g.desc: * The ~{BTN} is the general purpose User Button. It can be used by the host for any purpose.

g.text: The MCU can be configured to capture when it's pressed and perform operations (e.g. notify

g.desc: The MCU can be configured to capture when it's pressed and perform operations (e.g. notify

g.text: the host, change PDOs, etc. Because it's connected to BQ25620's ~QON}, it also gets the board

g.desc: the host, change PDOs, etc. Because it's connected to BQ25620's ~QON}, it also gets the board

g.text: off Ship mode (if battery is present), or force a reset if pressed for 8.5 seconds.

g.desc: off Ship mode (if battery is present), or force a reset if pressed for 8.5 seconds.
